column3: Viral rumor claims the city will outlaw home gardens next month.
Fabricated post says the governor admitted the vote totals were invented.
Hoax article says schools were ordered to replace water with soda.
False chain message claims the central bank will erase savings accounts.
Blog says officials secretly tracked every ballot by GPS chip.
False post says the mayor cancelled all ambulances for weekend protests.
Rumor claims the state shut down every hospital in one night.
Viral story says the tax agency will arrest anyone using cash tomorrow.
Official budget report showed tax receipts increased over the previous year.
State health officials confirmed the vaccination rate rose above ninety percent.
The labor department reported unemployment claims fell for a second week.
Auditors found the transit project finished under its revised budget target.
The court filing confirmed the settlement amount listed in the public record.
Education officials announced teacher vacancies dropped after the hiring program.
The census bureau reported population growth slowed in rural counties.
Emergency managers said the storm shelters opened before landfall.
